## Issue

Re-split of #10340 per maintainer feedback (no separate tracking issue exists). **PR 2 of 2.** Carries the user-facing behavior and the changeset.

**Stack (review/merge in order):** #11218 → #11219 (this PR). Stacked on #11218 — until that merges to `main`, this PR's diff also includes the validation commit.

This PR combines **path parsing** and **UI rendering/click**. They can't be separate PRs: the path-parsing API change and its only consumers (`marked.tsx`, `message-part.tsx`) don't compile apart, and the UI also depends on `data.validateFiles` introduced in #11218. Splitting them produced PRs that failed `typecheck` (`marked.tsx` importing the removed `parseFilePath`), so they're combined here to keep CI green.

## Context

The actual clickable-file-links UX, on top of the validation layer from #11218. Inline code that names a real workspace file becomes a clickable link that opens the file at the referenced line/column; non-file code stays plain.

## Implementation

**Path parsing**
- Replaces the regex `parseFilePath` with `extractSuffix` (strips `:line` / `:line:col` / `:start-end` suffixes) and `normalizeCandidatePath` (adds `./` to bare relative paths, strips `a/`+`b/` diff prefixes, leaves absolute/Windows/UNC paths alone).
- `extractFilePathFromHref` now returns `{ path, line, column }` instead of a bare string, preserving `file://` decoding and Windows-drive handling.
- Updated in both `packages/ui/src/file-path.ts` (shared opencode path, keeps its `kilocode_change` marker) and `packages/kilo-ui/src/file-path.ts` (Kilo-only).

**Rendering / click**
- The markdown `codespan` renderer marks every span as a `file-link-candidate`; the `link` renderer tags file-path hrefs with a `file-path-link` class for styling.
- `message-part.tsx` runs a post-render validation pass that calls `validateFiles` and promotes confirmed files to `.file-link` (cached so streaming re-renders resolve instantly).
- Important detail for reviewers: `Markdown` writes its DOM asynchronously (rAF-coalesced morphdom), so a synchronous effect scanned before the spans existed and never promoted anything. The pass is driven by a `MutationObserver` instead, which also covers streaming and de-dupes bursts to one pass per frame; `promote()` only touches classes/attributes, which `childList` observation ignores, so it can't loop.
- Clicking a confirmed link (or a markdown link whose href is a file path) opens the file at line/column; unconfirmed candidates stay plain.
- Adds file-path link styling and the changeset.

## Screenshots / Video

> Required — please attach. (Behavior was verified manually; capture from your dev extension.)

| before | after |
|---|---|
|  |  |

Suggested captures: (1) existing file references rendered as clickable links, (2) non-file inline code remaining plain, (3) a click opening the referenced file/line, (4) one example during streaming (morphdom/cache).

## How to Test

### Manual/local verification

- Human-executed (PR author) in the dev extension on this repo: real-file code spans become clickable and open at the right line/column; non-paths (`const n = 42`, `npm run watch`, `useState`) stay plain; a non-existent path stays plain; a broken markdown link shows "File not found"; an ambiguous link opens a quick-pick; links resolve correctly while a response streams.
- Agent-executed: `bun test src/file-path.test.ts` in `packages/ui/` (48 pass); `bun test tests/unit/kilo-ui-contract.test.ts` in `packages/kilo-vscode/` (28 pass); `ui` + `kilo-vscode` (extension + webview) `typecheck`; root `lint` (0 errors); `knip` (clean); `prettier --check` (clean); and an `esbuild` bundle build.

### Reviewer test steps

1. Build/launch the extension on a workspace with known files.
2. Ask the agent to reference real files as inline code with optional `:line` / `:line:col` / `:start-end`, plus some non-path code and a non-existent path.
3. Confirm only the real files are clickable and open at the right location; non-paths/non-existent stay plain.
4. Use a markdown `[text](missing.ts)` link to confirm the "File not found" warning, and `[text](dir/index.ts)` to confirm the multi-match quick-pick.
5. Ask for a long, streamed response that repeats real file paths; confirm links resolve and stay stable as it streams.

### Blocked checks and substitute verification

- Screenshots/video must be attached by a human; behavior was verified manually but images can't be captured from the agent environment.
- The full-monorepo pre-push typecheck fails locally only on `@kilocode/kilo-jetbrains` (JDK 21 not installed) — unrelated; CI runs the full typecheck.

## Checklist

- [x] Issue linked above, or exception explained
- [x] Tests/verification described
- [ ] Screenshots/video included for visual changes, or marked N/A — **to attach before marking ready**
- [x] Changeset considered for user-facing changes
- [x] I personally reviewed the diff and can explain the changes, including any AI-assisted work.

commits.messageBody:
Replace the regex-based parseFilePath with extractSuffix and
normalizeCandidatePath helpers, and make extractFilePathFromHref return
structured { path, line, column } data instead of a bare string.

This shifts inline-code detection from "does this string look like a
file?" to "extract a candidate path so the filesystem can confirm it
later", which also handles :line / :line:col and :start-end line ranges,
a/ and b/ diff prefixes, and Windows drive paths. Unit tests are
rewritten to cover the new helpers and the structured href result.

Add a validateFiles request/response round-trip between the webview and
the extension so the webview can confirm which inline code-span
candidates are real files before promoting them to clickable links.

The extension stat-checks candidate paths (new file-links.ts) and
replies with the subset that exist. Routing lives in editor-actions
alongside the other editor open actions, and openFile now falls back to
a workspace filename search (single match opens, multiple prompts) with
a "File not found" warning when a clicked path cannot be resolved.

commits.messageBody:
Mark every inline code span as a file-link candidate during markdown
rendering, then validate candidates against the filesystem after render
and promote confirmed files to clickable links. Results are cached so
morphdom re-renders during streaming resolve without extra round-trips,
and unconfirmed candidates fall back to plain code.

Clicking a confirmed link (or a markdown link whose href is a file path)
opens the file at the referenced line/column. Markdown file-path links
also get distinct styling.

commits.messageBody:
Address kilo-code-bot review on #11218: contains() is purely lexical, so a
checked-in symlink (e.g. root/link -> /etc) would pass the check and let
validateFiles stat an external target. validateFiles now resolves each
candidate's real path (symlinks included) and requires it to stay inside the
real session root before any stat, on top of the existing lexical pre-check
that still rejects UNC / absolute-outside / ../ without touching the disk.

11359.body:
Agent Manager currently has no dedicated path to start or continue Cloud Agent runs. The earlier integration in #10845 established the extension-host transport and UI, but it drifted from current Agent Manager code and relied on facade routes that are not available.

This rebases that work onto current `main` and adds an experimental `CLOUD AGENTS` category above local session history, scoped to the active repository. Users can discover existing runs, start a new cloud session using supported cloud modes and Kilo models, open it in an ephemeral tab, stream transcript updates, send plain-text follow-ups, and stop the remote run. Credentials remain in the extension host, cloud tabs do not enter local worktree state, and reconnect or authentication transitions rehydrate open tabs.

The cloud backend remains the source of truth for session identity and metadata. Agent Manager keeps only ephemeral open-tab and selection state, refreshes discovery when the section is expanded, and does not persist cloud sessions in `agent-manager.json`. The integration uses the existing local `/kilo/cloud-sessions` proxy for repository-filtered discovery and only calls facade endpoints it supports: session detail and messages, global events, `prompt_async`, and abort. Slash commands, attachments, custom modes and providers, and local-only actions remain disabled for this walking skeleton.

Enable **Cloud Agents** in Kilo Settings under **Experimental**, or set `experimental.cloud_agent` in `kilo.json`.

Supersedes #10845.

10845.body:
## Summary
- Add a signed-in Agent Manager Cloud Agents section so users can discover existing cloud sessions and attach them as ephemeral tabs without persisting them into local worktree state.
- Route cloud session list, transcript, stream, follow-up, and abort traffic through an extension-host controller with bounded credential refresh and reconnect handling, keeping bearer tokens out of the webview.
- Add a localhost Cloud Agent credential envelope endpoint, generated SDK surface, localized UI copy, restricted cloud composer, and behavioral coverage across the CLI and VS Code extension.

## Constraints
- This is a walking skeleton: cloud tabs intentionally retain raw session IDs and remain ephemeral while the UX is validated.
- Manual end-to-end smoke depends on the external Cloud Agent facade accepting `model` and `agent` follow-up overrides.

comments.body:
**Verdict**
I would not merge [#10845 `feat(agent-manager): add cloud agent session tabs`](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/pull/10845) yet.

The architectural direction is good and fits the extension better than a parallel standalone Cloud Agents panel would. The PR keeps credentials out of the browser webview, keeps cloud tabs ephemeral, and reuses the existing Agent Manager chat surface. However, it currently has one CI blocker and several reliability gaps around initial connection, reconnect recovery, and unsupported interactive requests.

**Design Trail**
These three earlier PRs explain the main architectural choices behind #10845:

1. [#6164 `docs: document product architecture and Agent Manager in AGENTS.md`](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/pull/6164)  
   This codified the current topology:
   - Agent Manager is an editor-tab feature inside the VS Code extension, not a separate runtime.
   - Sidebar chat, editor tabs, and Agent Manager share one `KiloConnectionService`.
   - That service owns one local `kilo serve` child process, one localhost SDK client, and one localhost SSE connection.
   - Worktree isolation comes from passing a `directory`, not from spawning a backend per session.

   #10845 follows this correctly. It does not spawn another local backend for Cloud Agents.

2. [#6198 `feat: cloud session browse-then-clone flow`](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/pull/6198)  
   This established the cloud-session API layering:
   - Kilo-specific cloud capabilities are exposed through localhost CLI routes.
   - The OpenAPI contract and generated SDK move together.
   - The extension host orchestrates cloud operations.
   - The webview receives projected data through typed messages.
   - Historical synced cloud sessions stay ephemeral until explicitly imported into local storage.

   #10845 intentionally adds a different cloud mode. These are live Cloud Agent attachments, not historical browse-then-clone previews. They remain ephemeral tabs and retain their remote session IDs.

3. [#7960 `feat(vscode): add KiloClaw chat panel to VS Code extension`](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/pull/7960)  
   This is the closest precedent for the privileged realtime boundary:
   - The extension host owns the remote authenticated connection.
   - The browser webview receives safe projected state through `postMessage`.
   - Tokens and privileged network clients stay in the Node.js extension host.

   #10845 applies the same principle inside Agent Manager rather than adding another standalone panel.

A supporting precedent is [#6785 `feat(kilo-vscode): add type safety infrastructure and typed message contracts`](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/pull/6785). #10845 extends those typed Agent Manager message unions rather than adding ad hoc payloads.

**Architecture Review**
The new flow is sensible:

```text
Agent Manager webview
  -> typed cloud session requests
AgentManagerProvider
  -> CloudAgentController in extension host
  -> local protected GET /kilo/cloud-agent/credentials
  -> direct SDK client against Cloud Agent facade
  -> projected sessions, transcripts, and SSE events back to webview
```

The local endpoint in `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/server/httpapi/groups/kilo-gateway.ts:347` returns a credential envelope containing `token`, `expiresAt`, and `kiloFacadeUrl`. It remains protected by the local CLI API authorization middleware at `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/server/httpapi/groups/kilo-gateway.ts:403`.

The extension-host controller then creates a direct remote SDK client in `packages/kilo-vscode/src/agent-manager/cloud-agent-controller.ts:164`. The bearer is never sent into browser context. This is the right security boundary.

Keeping the remote controller outside `KiloConnectionService` is also reasonable for this first slice. `KiloConnectionService` should continue to own the shared localhost `kilo serve` lifecycle. The Cloud Agent facade has a different origin, bearer, reconnect policy, and Agent Manager-specific lifetime. Shared SSE supervision can be extracted, but the remote lifecycle should remain explicit.

**Blocking Findings**
1. **CI blocker: the new HttpApi route is missing from the exerciser registry.**  
   The PR adds `GET /kilo/cloud-agent/credentials`, but `packages/opencode/test/kilocode/server/httpapi-exercise-scenarios.ts:159` jumps from `/kilo/claw/chat-credentials` directly to `/kilo/cloud-sessions`.

   Current Linux CI fails with:

   ```text
   Missing scenarios
   MISS GET /kilo/cloud-agent/credentials
   one or more routes have no scenario
   ```

   Add a protected unauthorized scenario beside the existing KiloClaw credential scenario. The dedicated route tests are useful, but they do not satisfy the route coverage gate.

2. **Initial Cloud Agents discovery can get stuck after a normal first-open race or after sign-in.**  
   `packages/kilo-vscode/webview-ui/agent-manager/AgentManagerApp.tsx:1456` calls `cloud.request()` once during mount. The controller fetches credentials through `this.connectionService.getClient()` in `packages/kilo-vscode/src/agent-manager/AgentManagerProvider.ts:101`, but `getClient()` throws until the shared localhost backend reaches `connected` in `packages/kilo-vscode/src/services/cli-backend/connection-service.ts:129`.

   This is a realistic race because the nested Agent Manager provider starts the local connection asynchronously while the webview mounts. When credential loading fails, `packages/kilo-vscode/src/agent-manager/cloud-agent-token.ts:32` adds a five-second cooldown. Nothing automatically retries when:
   - the local backend finishes connecting,
   - the local backend reconnects,
   - a signed-out user finishes device authentication.

   The only recovery is the manual Retry button, which can fail again during the cooldown window.

   The cloud list should load reactively when local-backend readiness and Kilo authentication transition to ready. A signed-out state should not be treated as an ordinary transient request failure.

3. **Remote SSE can remain silently stale forever after a half-open connection.**  
   `packages/kilo-vscode/src/agent-manager/cloud-agent-controller.ts:338` consumes the remote stream with an indefinite `for await`. Reconnect only occurs when the stream ends or throws.

   The existing localhost adapter already solves this in `packages/kilo-vscode/src/services/cli-backend/sdk-sse-adapter.ts:128`: it creates a per-attempt abort controller and resets a heartbeat watchdog on each SSE event. The Cloud Agent facade is remote and therefore more exposed to proxies, suspended sockets, and zombie TCP connections than localhost.

   Reuse or generalize the existing heartbeat supervision. Without it, transcript updates can silently stop while the tab still appears attached.

4. **Attach and reconnect hydrate transcript data but not authoritative session status.**  
   `packages/kilo-vscode/src/agent-manager/cloud-agent-controller.ts:227` loads `session.get()` and `session.messages()`. Reconnect at `packages/kilo-vscode/src/agent-manager/cloud-agent-controller.ts:333` calls the same loader. Neither path fetches `session.status()`.

   This creates two visible failure modes:
   - Opening an already-running Cloud Agent tab initially renders as idle until a future status event arrives.
   - Missing an `idle` event during disconnect can leave the composer permanently showing Stop after reconnect.

   The local extension already seeds status through `packages/kilo-vscode/src/session-status.ts:25`. Cloud Agent attachment needs equivalent status hydration on initial open and reconnect.

5. **Unsupported interactive requests can leave the remote run blocked indefinitely.**  
   `packages/kilo-vscode/src/agent-manager/cloud-agent-controller.ts:373` intercepts `permission.asked`, `question.asked`, and `suggestion.shown`, posts an error, and drops the event.

   If the remote agent is waiting for one of those responses, the run remains busy with no recovery path. For the walking skeleton, either:
   - prevent remote modes that can emit interactive requests,
   - explicitly reject or abort unsupported requests,
   - or surface a durable blocked state with an abort action.

   A transient error alone is not sufficient.

**Fit Issues**
- `packages/kilo-vscode/webview-ui/src/components/chat/ChatView.tsx:330` hides `StartupErrorBanner` for cloud tabs, but `packages/kilo-vscode/webview-ui/src/components/chat/CloudPromptInput.tsx:52` still disables sending when the local backend is disconnected. A cloud composer can therefore become inert without showing the normal recovery control.
- `packages/kilo-vscode/src/agent-manager/cloud-agent-controller.ts:72` replaces the sidebar list with `sessions: []` during every refresh and error. Preserve the last successful list so transient facade failures do not erase otherwise usable rows.
- Discovery is capped at `limit: 100` in `packages/kilo-vscode/src/agent-manager/cloud-agent-controller.ts:210`. That is acceptable for a walking skeleton, but it needs pagination before broad rollout.
- The raw Kilo bearer mirrors the KiloClaw precedent, but a scoped short-lived Cloud Agent capability would be safer before production expansion. Also validate that `kiloFacadeUrl` is HTTPS outside explicit development overrides.

**Maintainability**
The main architectural seam that will become fragile is the new `CLOUD = "cloud-agent"` string sentinel.

`SidebarSelection` remains `typeof LOCAL | string | null` in `packages/kilo-vscode/webview-ui/agent-manager/AgentManagerApp.tsx:160`. Historically, most non-`LOCAL` strings mean “worktree ID.” #10845 adds many individual `sel === CLOUD` exclusions across tab handling, worktree actions, keyboard shortcuts, diff routing, and empty states.

That is manageable for the walking skeleton, but it will be easy to miss a worktree assumption as Cloud Agents gain capabilities. A discriminated selection model would age better:

```ts
type SidebarSelection =
  | { kind: "local" }
  | { kind: "cloud" }
  | { kind: "worktree"; id: string }
  | { kind: "unassigned" }
```

A smaller intermediate step would be central `isCloudSelection()` and `isWorktreeSelection()` helpers used by every action path.

**Bottom Line**
The feature belongs in the current extension and the API split is directionally correct:

- Keep the local credential-envelope endpoint and generated SDK surface.
- Keep credentials and remote streaming in the extension host.
- Keep Cloud Agent tabs ephemeral and outside `.kilo/agent-manager.json`.
- Keep reusing `SessionProvider`, `ChatView`, and typed webview messages.
- Add connection-aware discovery retries, SSE heartbeat supervision, status hydration, and explicit unsupported-interaction handling before merging.

I reviewed the draft read-only and did not post GitHub comments. Current CI is failing only on the missing HttpApi exerciser scenario; the other reported issues come from code-path review rather than failing checks.

11510.body:
## Summary

Two CLI bug fixes:

1. **MiMo reasoning_effort: 'none' error** (#11501): For `@ai-sdk/openai-compatible` providers, user-provided variants with `{ reasoning: { effort: 'none' } }` are now remapped to `{ reasoning: { enabled: false } }` so the SDK never sends an unsupported effort value.

2. **MCP server toggle not persisting** (#6157): The `connect()` and `disconnect()` functions only modified in-memory state. Now both persist the `enabled` field to the project config file via `cfgSvc.update()`.

## Changes

### Fix 1: Reasoning effort remapping
- **`packages/opencode/src/provider/transform.ts`**: For `@ai-sdk/openai-compatible` models with user-provided variants, remap `reasoning: { effort: 'none' }` → `reasoning: { enabled: false }` (preserving sibling options via spread).
- **`packages/opencode/test/provider/cf-ai-gateway-e2e.test.ts`**: Two test cases covering basic remapping and sibling option preservation.

### Fix 2: MCP toggle persistence

11511.body:
## Summary

When a user rejects a file read (or any tool permission), the agent should continue without the rejected tool call, not halt entirely. The previous default was to stop the agent loop on any rejection, which is almost never the desired behavior.

## Changes

- **`packages/opencode/src/session/processor.ts`**: Changed `shouldBreak` default from `true` to `false`. The agent now continues the loop when a permission is rejected, unless `experimental.stop_on_deny` is explicitly set to `true`.
- **`packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts`**: Added `stop_on_deny` config option (default `false`). Deprecated `continue_loop_on_deny` in favor of the more intuitive `stop_on_deny`.

## Verification

- `bun test test/session/processor-effect.test.ts` — all 14 tests pass
- `bun test test/permission-task.test.ts` — all 8 tests pass
- `bun test test/cli/run/permission.shared.test.ts` — all 18 tests pass
- Pre-existing test timeout in `session-prompt-permission-refresh.test.ts` is unrelated (fails on main too)

## Testing Evidence

All existing processor and permission tests continue to pass. The behavior change is minimal: only the default value of `shouldBreak` flips from `true` to `false`. Users who want the old behavior can set `experimental.stop_on_deny: true` in their config.

Fixes #7338

Good catch on the backward compat. Fixed in the latest commit — `continue_loop_on_deny: true` now explicitly sets `shouldBreak = false`, preserving the existing behavior for users who have it configured. The default behavior (when neither flag is set) is now `shouldBreak = false` (don't halt on deny), and `stop_on_deny: true` opts in to the old halt-on-deny behavior.
Changeset added (`permission-continue-on-deny.md`) documenting the user-facing behavior change and the `stop_on_deny` opt-out.
